title: HSQC_JMOD_15N_3D.nan
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"50qeo","text":"This  protocol describes running a pseudo-3D J-modulated 15N HSQC for measuring backbone 1JH,N scalar and 1DH,N dipolar couplings.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":57,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":89,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":106,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":91,"length":3},{"style":"sub","offset":108,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lrso","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eghc8","text":"This pulse sequence can be used for measurement of 1H-15N residual dipolar couplings (RDCs) in large proteins","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c30bd","text":"15N isotope labeling is required;  can also be applied to proteins with additional 13C and 2H labeling.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":0,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":83,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":91,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"97l9i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"434es","text":"It uses the custom pulseprogram 'hsqcetfpf3jcgpsi2.nan' derived from the standard Topspin library version.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2vhd5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6vbjc","text":"Number of directly acquired points (2 TD) should be set to keep acquisition time t2,max (2 AQ) ~50-120 ms. For large proteins (~25 kDa) set closer to the lower range, higher values for small proteins.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1erij","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6dvja","text":"For samples with 13C labeling use -DLABEL_CN flag to enable 13C decoupling during 15N evolution. 13C channel offset O2P should be set ~110 ppm (middle of 13C aliphatic and 13CO shift range).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ef4c3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3vmkh","text":"A sample must be inserted in the magnet either locally by the user after training, or by facility staff if running remotely.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6d9li","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5dk42","text":"This protocol requires a sample is locked, tuned/matched on 1H, 13C and 15N channels, and  shimmed. At a minimum, 1H 90° pulse width and offset O1 should be calibrated and a 1D proton spectrum with water suppression has been collected according to the protocol PRESAT_bio.nan. Prior acquisition of  a 2D 15N HSQC is also recommended, according to protocol  HSQC_15N.nan.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":261,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":301,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":357,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0ee5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cauaj","text":"General aspects of the use of the bioTop module are described in more detail in the protocols listed below.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":33,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"af3n1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"au92p","text":"It is recommended to calibrate 1H carrier offset, 1H H2O selective flip-back pulse, as well as 1H, 13C, and 15N 90° pulse widths using the \"Optimization\" tab of BioTop. Alternatively, 1H 90° pulse width and offset can be calibrated using other methods, such as pulsecal or calibo1p1. Additional parameters, like 15N and 13C offsets and spectral width can be either optimized or manually entered in the \"Optimization\" tab of BioTop. Note that since BioTop optimizations are saved in the dataset folder, all experiments should be created under the same dataset name when using BioTop for acquisition setup.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anhdc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"auhhr","text":"Refer to protocols ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"51f75","text":" Acquisition Setup Workflow, Solution NMR Structural Biology","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ho3e","text":"PRESAT_bio.nan","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4gbba","text":"HSQC_15N.nan","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5q2hl","text":"HNCO_3D.nan","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ltiv","text":"Biotop-Calibration and Acquisition setup","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8bqg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Start with existing Dataset containing 1D proton or 15N-HSQC data for this sample.
Open create dataset window with edc command or through menu. Edit text in Title box.
When Lock, Tune, Shim are complete proceed to the specific protocol for your desired pulseprogram(s).

To select starting parameter set, check 'Read parameterset' box, and click Select. For standard NAN parameter sets, change the Source directory at upper right corner of the window: Source = /opt/NAN_SB/par Click 'Select' to bring up list of parameter sets. Select HSQC_JMOD_15N_3D_xxx.par, where xxx=900,800 or 600. Click OK at bottom of window to create the new EXPNO directory.

Loading the HSQC_JMOD_15N_3D_xxx.par parameter set enters the default parameters into the experiment directory. While they represent a good starting point, they may not be fully optimal or accurate for your particular sample or spectrometer hardware. The probe- and solvent-specific parameters, specifically the 1H 90° pulse length, and possibly the 13C and 15N 90° pulse lengths, along with other dependent pulse widths and powers may need to be updated. There are two ways of automatically updating experimental parameters: 1) Use getprosol command ( step 2.1), which typically only updates proton pulse widths and power levels. It is most useful for running routine experiments using the default parameters. 2) Or use bioTop module that organizes calibrated and defined parameters for a dataset.

Type ' rga' or click on 'Gain' in Topspin Acquire menu to execute automatic gain adjustment. Type 'expt' to calculate the expected run time Type ' zg ' or click on 'Run' in Topspin Acquire menu to begin acquisition.

You can always check the first FID by typing 'efp' to execute an exponential multiplied Fourier transform. It will ask for a FID #, choose the default #1. You can evaluate the 1D spectrum for amide proton signal to noise and water suppression.

Use bioTop If you previously performed parameter calibrations using the "Optimizations" tab of the BioTop GUI, or entered parameters manually in the "Optimizations" tab, you can type btprep at the command line. See protocol 'BioTop : calibration and acquisition parameters' and attached Bruker manual 'biotop.pdf' for details.
If not done, tune Nitrogen and Carbon channels: -on the Acquire menu, click Tune ( or type atma on command line).

Using getprosol: Use the calibrated proton P1 value obtained from the proton experiment ( protocol PRESAT_bio.nan) and note the standard power level attenuation in dB for P1 (PLW1); otherwise type calibo1p1 and wait till finished. Then execute the getprosol command: getprosol 1H [ calibrated P1 value] [power level for P1] e.g. getprosol 1H 9.9 -13.14. Where for example, the calibrated P1=9.9 at power level -13.14 dB attenuation This also loads 15N and 13C pulse widths and power levels from the 'edprosol' table, and are assumed to be sufficiently accurate. Skip to step 2.3.

Then examine the parameters in Acqpars 'ased' mode (click 'pulse' icon), or type 'ased' ; Most of the default parameters are suitable, however it's useful to compare values in the fields against those proposed by the original parameter file and the pulseprogram. CNST4: 1 J NH value (≥93 Hz, to calculate INEPT transfer delays, can be optimized using popt in 1D mode to maximize S/N) D1: recyle delay (~1 s for protonated samples, ~2-3 s for perdeuterated samples D24: 1/(4*J NH ), normally set to same value as D28 DS: 32-128 'dummy' scans that are not recorded ; allows system to reach steady state equilibration. NS: multiple of 4; increase for increased signal to noise ( S/N increases as √NS ) ZGOPTNS flags -DLABEL_CN : enable 13C decoupling during 15N evolution for 13C,15N-labeled samples (default) VDLIST: J-coupling evolution delay list. Delays should be no smaller than (p21*1.273+20u) P1 - 1H 90º high power pulse (calibrated with calibo1p1 or BioTop) SPdB1 - power level [dB] for 1H H2O flip-back shaped pulse (calibrated with BioTop) P3 - 13C 90º high power pulse (calibrated with BioTop) P21 - 15N 90º high power pulse (calibrated with BioTop)

Examine parameters by typing 'eda' , or select the 'Acqpars' tab and get the 'eda' window by clicking on the ' A ' icon. This view shows the two dimensions, F2 (1H) and F2 (15N) in columns. Parameters to check: 3 SW: 1H spectral width (~12-15 ppm, defined in BioTop) 2 SW: 15N amide spectral width (~25-40 ppm, defined in BioTop) O1P: 1H offset defaults to ~4.7ppm ( calibrated with bioTop) O2P: 13C offset (~110 ppm, for decoupling when ZGOPTNS -DLABEL_CN ) O3P: 15N amide offset (~115-120 ppm, defined in bioTop) 3 TD: Number of 1H time domain real points (~1024-2048, preferably 2 N , keep 3 AQ at ~50-120 ms) 2 TD: Number of 15 N time domain real points (keep 2 AQ at ~30-60 ms) 1 TD: Number of delays in VDLIST
2D planes with NUS or uniformly sampled can be processed with Topspin: xfb
creator: NAN-KB UGA
